---
title: Ameer Dharamshi
subtitle: | 
 | PhD Candidate, Department of Biostatistics, University of Washington 

about:
  id: head
  template: trestles
  image: files/profile_UW.jpeg
  image-width: 15em
  image-shape: round
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:adharams@uw.edu
    - icon: mortarboard-fill
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=DhpLxlQAAAAJ&hl=en&oi=ao
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/AmeerD
    - icon: linkedin
      text: Linkedin
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/ameerdharamshi
  # - icon: person-vcard-fill
    #   text: ORCID
    #   href: https://orcid.org/
     
---

### About Me

I am a fourth-year PhD Candidate in the Department of Biostatistics at the University of Washington supervised by Professors Daniela Witten and Jon Wakefield. In August 2026 I will be joining the Department of Mathematics and Statistics at McGill University as an Assistant Professor. My research focuses on developing strategies for selective inference and predictive model uncertainty quantification that are applicable in complex data settings and/or with small sample sizes. I also actively work on collaborative projects in demography, global development, and quantitative social science that aim to estimate various population indicators (e.g., mortality, fertility, and school completion rates) at national and subnational levels.

Previously, I worked as a research and data consultant for the [Global Education Monitoring Report](https://en.unesco.org/gem-report/) at UNESCO. I completed my Master of Science in Statistical Sciences at the University of Toronto and my Bachelor of Mathematics at the University of Waterloo. 

For more about my academic and professional experiences, please see my [Research](pages/publications.qmd) and [CV](pages/cv.qmd). I am always looking for interesting collaborations so if you have a project in mind, please do feel free to reach out!

Outside of my work, I like to ice skate, play squash, run, bake, and really enjoy a [good book](pages/reading.qmd).

<!-- ### Recent News -->

<!-- ::: {.content-hidden unless-format="markdown"} -->
<!-- news_update_start -->
<!-- ::: -->

<!-- Published paper **[Paper 3](https://doi.org)** in Journal 3 -->

<!-- Released preprint  **[Paper 4](https://arxiv.org/)** -->

<!-- Exciting update here -->

<!-- Presented poster **[Poster](https://github.com)** at Conference -->

<!-- Published paper **[Paper 2](https://github.com)** in Journal 2 -->

<!-- ::: {.content-hidden unless-format="markdown"} -->
<!-- news_update_end -->
<!-- ::: -->
